---
title: "WorldcupGit"
output: html_document
date: "2026-06-05"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(footBayes)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)
library(posterior)
library(bayesplot)
library(loo)
library(cmdstanr)
library(instantiate)
# ============================================================
# 1. Load data
# ============================================================
getwd()
setwd("C:/Users/Rayen/Downloads") ## Attention
worldcup <- read.csv("results.csv")


worldcup2026 <- worldcup %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(date)) %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2022-01-01")) %>%
  mutate(is_future = is.na(home_score) | is.na(away_score)) %>%
  arrange(is_future, date)

summary(worldcup2026)
table(worldcup2026$is_future)


```



## R Markdown



```{r cars}


# ============================================================
# Teams that played friendly matches


friendly_teams <- worldcup2026 %>%
  filter(tournament == "Friendly") %>%
  select(home_team, away_team) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(home_team, away_team),
    names_to = "side",
    values_to = "team"
  ) %>%
  distinct(team) %>%
  arrange(team)

friendly_teams

```

## 

Friendly matches adjustments


```{r }



# ============================================================
# Friendly matches adjustments
# ============================================================

library(dplyr)

# Teams present in the WC 2026
wc48_teams <- worldcup2026 %>%
  filter(is_future == TRUE) %>%
  select(home_team, away_team)

wc48_teams <- unique(c(wc48_teams$home_team, wc48_teams$away_team))

length(wc48_teams) # should be 48 
wc48_teams


# ============================================================
# Check NAs in non-future matches

played_matches <- worldcup2026 %>%
  filter(is_future == FALSE)

# Show rows where at least one column has NA
played_matches_with_na <- played_matches %>%
  filter(if_any(everything(), is.na))


# Number of played matches with at least one NA
nrow(played_matches_with_na)


#Add score variables and flags only once
worldcup2026_flagged <- worldcup2026 %>%
  mutate(
    total_goals = ifelse(is_future == FALSE, home_score + away_score, NA_real_),
    goal_diff   = ifelse(is_future == FALSE, abs(home_score - away_score), NA_real_),

    both_teams_in_wc48 = home_team %in% wc48_teams &
      away_team %in% wc48_teams,

    friendly_suspect = tournament == "Friendly" &
      is_future == FALSE &
      (
        goal_diff >= 5 |
          (total_goals >= 7 & goal_diff >= 4)
      ),

    remove_match = friendly_suspect & !both_teams_in_wc48
  )

# Inspect suspicious friendly matches

friendly_suspects <- worldcup2026_flagged %>%
  filter(friendly_suspect == TRUE) %>%
  select(
    date,
    home_team,
    away_team,
    home_score,
    away_score,
    tournament,
    total_goals,
    goal_diff,
    both_teams_in_wc48,
    remove_match,
    neutral
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(goal_diff), desc(total_goals))



# Inspect matches that will actually be removed
matches_to_remove <- worldcup2026_flagged %>%
  filter(remove_match == TRUE) %>%
  select(
    date,
    home_team,
    away_team,
    home_score,
    away_score,
    tournament,
    total_goals,
    goal_diff,
    both_teams_in_wc48,
    remove_match,
    neutral
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(goal_diff), desc(total_goals))


# Remove only the selected matches
worldcup2026_clean <- worldcup2026_flagged %>%
  filter(remove_match == FALSE)


# on fait le checking
cat("Matches before cleaning:", nrow(worldcup2026), "\n")
cat("Matches after cleaning:", nrow(worldcup2026_clean), "\n")
cat("Removed matches:", nrow(worldcup2026) - nrow(worldcup2026_clean), "\n")

table(worldcup2026$is_future)
table(worldcup2026_clean$is_future)


```


# footBayes 



```{r}

# Prepare data for footBayes WITHOUT FIFA ranking (à ajouter aprés)


results_footBayes <- worldcup2026_clean %>%
  mutate(
    periods = as.numeric(format(date, "%Y"))
  ) %>%
  arrange(is_future, date) %>%
  rename(
    home_goals = home_score,
    away_goals = away_score
  ) %>%
  select(
    periods,
    home_team,
    away_team,
    home_goals,
    away_goals,
    is_future
  )

# ==============================
# Iteratively remove training rows where away_team is not present as home_team
# This avoids footBayes error: Variable 'team2' has NA values
# =========================

repeat {
  
  train_home_teams <- results_footBayes %>%
    filter(is_future == FALSE) %>%
    pull(home_team) %>%
    as.character() %>%
    unique()
  
  bad_away_train <- results_footBayes %>%
    filter(is_future == FALSE) %>%
    pull(away_team) %>%
    as.character() %>%
    unique() %>%
    setdiff(train_home_teams)
  
  if (length(bad_away_train) == 0) {
    break
  }
  
  cat("Bad away teams removed in this iteration:\n")
  print(bad_away_train)
  
  rows_before <- nrow(results_footBayes)
  
  results_footBayes <- results_footBayes %>%
    filter(
      is_future == TRUE |
        !(as.character(away_team) %in% bad_away_train)
    )
  
  rows_after <- nrow(results_footBayes)
  
  cat("Rows removed:", rows_before - rows_after, "\n")
}

# ============================================================
# Recompute number of future matches
# ============================================================

n_future <- sum(results_footBayes$is_future)

cat("Number of future matches:", n_future, "\n")

if (n_future != 72) {
  warning("The number of future matches is not 72 after filtering.")
}

# ============================================================
# Create data_model
# Replace future NA scores by artificial 11-11
# ============================================================

data_model <- results_footBayes %>%
  mutate(
    home_goals = ifelse(is.na(home_goals), 11, home_goals),
    away_goals = ifelse(is.na(away_goals), 11, away_goals)
  ) %>%
  select(
    periods,
    home_team,
    away_team,
    home_goals,
    away_goals
  )

# ============================================================
# Final checks
# ============================================================

cat("NA home_team:", sum(is.na(data_model$home_team)), "\n")
cat("NA away_team:", sum(is.na(data_model$away_team)), "\n")
cat("NA home_goals:", sum(is.na(data_model$home_goals)), "\n")
cat("NA away_goals:", sum(is.na(data_model$away_goals)), "\n")

n_total <- nrow(data_model)
n_train <- n_total - n_future

train_data <- data_model[1:n_train, ]
pred_data  <- data_model[(n_train + 1):n_total, ]

bad_away_train_check <- setdiff(
  unique(as.character(train_data$away_team)),
  unique(as.character(train_data$home_team))
)

bad_away_pred_check <- setdiff(
  unique(as.character(pred_data$away_team)),
  unique(as.character(train_data$home_team))
)

cat("Bad away teams in training after correction:\n")
print(bad_away_train_check)

cat("Bad away teams in prediction after correction:\n")
print(bad_away_pred_check)

tail(data_model, n_future)


```



```{r}












fit_and_predict_model <- function(model_name) {
  
  cat("Fitting model:", model_name, "\n")
  
  fit <- stan_foot(
    data = data_model,
    model = model_name,
    predict = n_future,
    dynamic_type = "seasonal",
    home_effect = FALSE,
    init = 0,
    chains = 4,
    iter_sampling = 1000,
    iter_warmup = 1000,
    seed = 123
  )
  
  cat("Computing predicted probabilities for:", model_name, "\n")
  
  prob <- foot_prob(fit, data_model)
  
  colnames(prob$prob_table) <- c(
    "home",
    "away",
    "home win",
    "draw",
    "away win",
    "mlo"
  )
  
  return(
    list(
      fit = fit,
      prob = prob
    )
  )
}


# Double Poisson model
# ============================================================

res_dp <- fit_and_predict_model(
  model_name = "double_pois"
)

knitr::kable(res_dp$prob$prob_table)

res_dp$prob$prob_plot




```



```{r}
# ============================================================
# Bivariate Poisson model
# ==========================================================



res_bp <- fit_and_predict_model(
  model_name = "biv_pois"
)

knitr::kable(res_bp$prob$prob_table)

res_bp$prob$prob_plot




```





```{r}
# ============================================================
# Diagonal-inflated Bivariate Poisson model
# ============================================================

res_dbp <- fit_and_predict_model(
  model_name = "diag_infl_biv_pois"
)

knitr::kable(res_dbp$prob$prob_table)

res_dbp$prob$prob_plot




```



```{r}





```



```{r}


```
